title_seed: Why Verified Tip Lines Matter for Newsrooms
scenario: A local newsroom wants sources to find the right public tip line without guessing which profile is legitimate.
prompt_angle: Explain how directory discovery and visible verification reduce first-contact trust problems for sources and reporters.

title_seed: How Law Firms Can Handle Sensitive Intake Without Losing Track
scenario: A small law office needs a secure intake workflow for sensitive outreach that may arrive at odd hours and require careful triage.
prompt_angle: Show how inbox organization and message statuses support real legal intake work without forcing a chat-style workflow.

title_seed: What Boards and Ethics Offices Should Put on a Tip Line Before Launch
scenario: A board or ethics office wants a public reporting channel that sets expectations clearly before a whistleblower types the first message.
prompt_angle: Center the practical importance of clear recipient guidance and profile setup before a public launch.

title_seed: Why Schools and Universities Need Separate Reporting Addresses
scenario: A university needs separate public intake paths for student safety concerns, Title IX-style reporting, and financial misconduct.
prompt_angle: Explain how aliases create clearer reporting lanes without forcing people to learn a complicated intake system.

title_seed: Small Usability Details That Matter When Reporting Under Stress
scenario: An organizer is checking sensitive disclosures from a phone in low-light or high-stress environments where comfort and readability matter more than teams admit.
prompt_angle: Frame dark mode and mobile usability as operational details that affect whether high-stress users stick with a secure workflow.

title_seed: How Security Teams Can Investigate Suspicious Email With Better Context
core_user_label: software developers and bug bounty teams
scenario: A security or bug bounty team receives suspicious forwarded material and needs a better first pass before escalating internally.
prompt_angle: Connect Hush Line's email-validation tool to real bug bounty or incident-response intake work.

title_seed: When OCR Helps Reporters Handle Documents Faster
scenario: A reporter receives photographed or scanned material and needs quick OCR to decide whether a disclosure is worth deeper follow-up.
prompt_angle: Explain how OCR fits into the first-pass workflow for investigative reporting rather than treating it as a generic AI feature.

title_seed: Why Data Export Matters for Long-Running Cases
scenario: A legal team needs to preserve its own operational records and keep disclosures portable as matters stretch over months or years.
prompt_angle: Center continuity, portability, and recordkeeping instead of abstract privacy language.

title_seed: How Recipients Can Get Encryption Working Faster With Proton Key Lookup
core_user_label: journalists, lawyers, and administrators setting up recipient accounts
scenario: A new recipient wants to get a secure intake page live quickly without fumbling through manual key setup on day one.
prompt_angle: Show how faster encryption setup reduces launch friction for real recipients who still need strong defaults.

title_seed: Who Should Run a Personal Server Tip Line
scenario: An independent journalist, lawyer, or organizer wants more direct control over their reporting system than a shared hosted service provides.
prompt_angle: Explain who the personal server path is for, what tradeoffs it introduces, and when that extra control is worth it.
